target.title: fix(capture): avoid false-success screen captures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a false-success capture path where legacy/CoreGraphics screen capture could report success while returning redacted wallpaper/blank pixels from non-Aqua/background launchd sessions.

This PR:

- requires the Screen Recording permission gate for legacy capture as well as ScreenCaptureKit capture
- prefers the system `/usr/sbin/screencapture` path for legacy full-screen capture
- converts `NSScreen.frame` into the top-left virtual desktop coordinate space expected by `screencapture -R`, including vertical display layouts
- fails closed for full-screen legacy capture if `screencapture -R` fails, instead of falling back to `CGDisplayCreateImage`
- shares the system `screencapture` process/decode helper across screen, area, and window capture
- adds `peekaboo permissions request-screen-recording` for the local Peekaboo process/current binary
- updates permission docs around Bridge vs local runtime, `--capture-engine` as a local-debug override, and Homebrew Cellar path/TCC staleness
- updates the Core regression test so legacy-first capture still checks Screen Recording permission

## Background / root cause

On the repro machine, the Codex shell runs under a Background launchd manager, while Terminal-launched `.command` scripts run in the active Aqua GUI session. The installed Homebrew Peekaboo 3.4.1 could return a successful classic capture from the Background session even though the screenshot pixels were only wallpaper/blank/redacted output.

That behavior is hazardous for automation because the caller receives success and an image file, but the image does not represent the visible desktop.

The fix makes legacy capture fail before attempting capture when Screen Recording is missing, and uses macOS's `screencapture -R` path for legacy screen pixels where available. If that full-screen system capture fails, the PR now refuses the unsafe `CGDisplayCreateImage` fallback so the same wallpaper-only false success cannot be reintroduced by the fallback path.

## Validation

Passed locally on current head `edb73a3a`:

```sh
git diff --check
swift build --package-path Apps/CLI
PEEKABOO_INCLUDE_AUTOMATION_TESTS=true swift test --package-path Apps/CLI --filter PermissionsCommandTests
swift test --package-path Core/PeekabooCore --filter ScreenCapturePlannerMatchDisplayTests
```

`PermissionsCommandTests` ran 4 Swift Testing tests successfully.

`ScreenCapturePlannerMatchDisplayTests` ran 25 Swift Testing tests successfully, including the new `screencapture -R` region conversion cases for primary, right, left, above, below, and mixed left+above display layouts.

Manual/live evidence gathered before this PR:

- installed Homebrew 3.4.1 from the Background launchd session returned success with wallpaper-only pixels
- direct Aqua `/usr/sbin/screencapture -x` captured the real multi-window desktop
- patched debug build through an Aqua Peekaboo Bridge on `/tmp/peekaboo-patched-bridge.sock` captured the real 1920x1080 multi-window desktop
- patched `see` through that bridge returned about 381 UI elements
- `inspect-ui --app-target 'iPhone Mirroring'` found the iPhone Mirroring Resume button / Connection Paused state
- `click` on that Resume element succeeded with `deliveryMode=background`

## Validation limitations

- A fresh post-commit bridge screenshot was attempted, but the macOS console was locked at that point (`IOConsoleLocked = Yes`), so the fresh capture returned a black frame. I did not use that as success evidence.
- The broader `swift test --package-path Core/PeekabooCore --filter ScreenCaptureServiceFlowTests` run was previously attempted, but the machine ran out of disk space while SwiftPM was building that package. The targeted planner regression suite now completes and passes on the current head.
- `swiftformat` was not run because `swiftformat` was not installed on `PATH` in this environment.

## Review follow-up

Addressed the ClawSweeper P1 fallback concern by changing full-screen legacy capture to fail closed when system `screencapture` fails. Area/window fallback behavior is unchanged; the wallpaper-only false-success class was observed on the full-screen `CGDisplayCreateImage` path.

Addressed the ClawSweeper P1 coordinate concern in `edb73a3a`: full-screen legacy capture now normalizes the selected `NSScreen.frame` against the union of all `NSScreen` frames and flips AppKit's lower-left Y coordinate into the top-left virtual desktop coordinate space expected by `screencapture -R`. A requested independent subagent review caught an earlier local version that only translated Y; that was corrected before pushing.

## Notes

`request-screen-recording` is documented as local-process/current-binary only. Unlike Event Synthesizing, this PR does not add a bridge protocol operation for remotely requesting Screen Recording on the bridge host.
